transcript:
DEMO CALL TRANSCRIPT - BEN'S ELECTRIC SOLUTIONS
Date: January 2026
Participants: Bharat (Clara Solutions Expert), Nick (Clara Team), Ben Penoyer (Owner - Ben's Electric Solutions)

---

Bharat: Ben, good morning. How are you?

Ben: I'm doing very well. How are you?

Bharat: Very well. Really excited about our conversation. So let me start off by introducing myself and my company. I represent the solutions team here at Clara. What I do day in and day out is speak to contractors such as yourself, understand your workflows, and see where we can plug in our solution to further enhance the way you do things.

Bharat: I'd love to know a little about you. How long have you been in this business?

Ben: I've been working for myself for about eight years now. I've been an electrician for 30 years, running my own business for the last eight. I'm trying to grow a little bit bigger this year. We've been relatively small. I just have a couple guys, couple vans out on the road. Do pretty much all the admin and back end stuff myself. As we get busier, I've got to get some help here. I've actually got another guy who's going to be coming on as an operations manager to help me out with all of that sort of stuff.

Bharat: So you have what, four people working for you today out in the field?

Ben: I've got two company work vans. I'm getting a third one on the road at the end of the week. So I'll have three company work vans and then I've got two subcontractors I use. About four vans on the road typically is what we're going to be operating. By the end of the year, I want to have five.

Bharat: You mentioned subcontractors. They come in for certain projects?

Ben: Yeah, pretty much. When we get busy I bring in subcontractors to take in the overflow work. I've had one guy working basically full time for me as a subcontractor. Subcontractors are great because they're generally pretty good electricians, but they're not as reliable because when they get their own jobs sometimes they don't get very much notice. That's why we're getting another company van on the road.

Bharat: So you have journeymen and apprentices?

Ben: Yeah, so each van has a full time journeyman. I've got three journeymen and then I'll have one apprentice. I just laid off an apprentice so I have to be getting another one here pretty soon.

Bharat: So all the other folks are usually out in the field and you're the one taking care of the entire office?

Ben: That's right, yeah.

Bharat: Do you go out in the field as well?

Ben: I do. I try not to need to be helping the guys, but generally I can help them over the phone. I go into the field to look at jobs and do quotes. A lot of customer facing stuff.

Bharat: What kind of jobs do you do? Service calls or projects?

Ben: Everything. We do service calls, small jobs, odd jobs. We get a lot of weird stuff that not a lot of contractors do. A lot of renovations, a lot of troubleshooting. And then we do get some full projects like new custom homes or tenant improvement in a building. We do EV chargers, hot tub hookups, panel changes, all that service maintenance stuff.

Bharat: You mentioned service calls. You're getting calls during hours, after hours, over the weekend, around the holidays?

Ben: We do, yeah. Most of them is business hours. But I do get the occasional call after hours and weekends. Sometimes I do turn some of that stuff down too, because we're not equipped for emergency services except for some of our regular property managers and general contractors. We'll get out there for emergency calls for them. But if someone has an emergency call on the weekend, I'll defer them elsewhere.

Bharat: Do you have somebody on call after hours?

Ben: I'm the one on call.

Bharat: So when you go out in the field during the day, it's all about managing the operations. And after hours is when you handle emergencies?

Ben: Yeah, and I don't have to very often. I've got one property manager, they run some gas stations. Sometimes when their pumps go down, they've got to be fixed now. And so I get out there and do that one because I don't have any of my guys on call.

Bharat: You mentioned you had an experience with a virtual assistant?

Ben: It was actually a virtual assistant, a live person that I had access to. I was paying them for 20 hours a week of her time. It was weird because I was only paying for a certain number of hours. I couldn't have her answering the phones necessarily. I probably could have used her more for client data entry. Someone in that position, I needed them to be more proactive and I was pretty new to my business then.

Ben: I have an assistant now who I'm going to be bringing on as an operations manager or estimator to help me with the stuff I'm doing. That's going way better. I communicate with him all the time, get him to do a lot of data entry for me. Next month I'm going to be getting him to go look at some jobs by himself.

Bharat: What CRM do you use?

Ben: I've got a software called Jobber that runs as my CRM and it's fantastic. It really helps out a lot.

Bharat: Any services you don't do anymore?

Ben: I was trying to do the gemstone lights for a little while, but that just wasn't worth it. We still get calls for that and have to say no, we don't do that. It's definitely good to have the guys trained in certain things. I can do just about anything with 30 years experience, but the younger guys don't have the same vision or experience, so they can't necessarily take everything on with the same knowledge.

Bharat: What about emergency handling for after hours?

Ben: We're not equipped for general emergency services. I'm the one on call. For our regular property managers and general contractors, we'll get out there for emergency calls for them. I've got one property manager that runs gas stations - when their pumps go down, they've got to be fixed immediately. But for random customers with emergencies on weekends, I'll defer them elsewhere to specialty services.

Bharat: What's your call volume like?

Ben: It varies. I probably get 10 to 20 calls a week from just small customers. Most of the calls I get are from my regular contractors. I also get calls from inspectors and from the guys on the tools. And then all the random sales calls too. Probably 20 to 50 calls a week total.

Bharat: How would you want Clara to handle different callers?

Ben: It'd be pretty cool to have Clara answer most of the business calls. I have a couple contractors who are kind of a bit of a pain, and it would be okay to have them talk to Clara. Clara can summarize what they had to say and then I can call them back. But I'd have quite a few numbers to just let go through - I've got a lot of contractors that are great and I definitely need to be talking to them right away. Those preferred contacts should just call me directly.

Bharat: We'll configure that during onboarding. Personal numbers and VIP customers won't be screened.

Ben: Perfect. I would like to try it out for sure. I kind of like the idea of giving it a three months try.

---

KEY DETAILS:
- Company: Ben's Electric Solutions
- Owner: Ben Penoyer
- Location: Calgary, Alberta, Canada
- Email: Ben@Benselectricsolutionsteam.com
- Experience: 30 years as electrician, 8 years own business
- Team: 3 journeymen, 1 apprentice (hiring), 2 subcontractors, operations manager coming on (Greg)
- Vehicles: 3 company vans, growing to 5 by end of year
- CRM: Jobber
- Accounting: QuickBooks (through Jobber)
- Services: Residential/commercial electrical, service calls, renovations, EV chargers, hot tub hookups, panel changes, custom homes, tenant improvements
- Do NOT do: Gemstone lights
- Emergency handling: Ben is on-call, handles emergencies ONLY for regular property managers/GCs (especially gas station pumps). Defer other emergencies elsewhere.
- Call volume: 20-50 calls/week
- Routing preference: VIP contractors go directly to Ben, pain contractors can be screened by Clara
